authors: Slavin KV, North RB, Deer TR, Staats P, Davis K, Diaz R
title: Tonic and burst spinal cord stimulation waveforms for chronic, intractable pain: study protocol for a randomized controlled trial
study_question: Is burst stimulation noninferior to tonic stimulation for chronic neuropathic pain after three months?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic neuropathic trunk or limb pain after successful tonic stimulation trial
follow_up_duration: 24 weeks controlled phase, then open-label follow-up every 6 months to 2 years
outcome_measures: VAS pain, responder rate, paresthesia, BDI-II, PCS, ODI, satisfaction, adverse events
inclusion_criteria: Age 22+, chronic neuropathic pain, baseline VAS 60+, failed 3 treatments, successful tonic trial
answer_to_study_question: Protocol paper only; results pending. Trial intended to test burst noninferiority versus tonic stimulation.
reasoning: Most fields showed strong agreement across all models. I chose consensus bibliographic data, PubMed link from OpenRouter and Google, and sample size 173 because Anthropic and Google agreed while OpenRouter had low confidence.
field_level_decisions_json: {"title": "Google title matched Anthropic closely and fits published protocol wording.", "volume_issue_pages": "Anthropic and Google agreed with high confidence on 17:569.", "number_of_participants_in_study": "Selected 173 over 76 due to two-model agreement and higher confidence.", "answer_to_study_question": "This is a protocol, so no outcome results are available."}
